summary: Conjecture 2 of arXiv:2308.15721 asserts that for every graph $H$, both the defective chromatic number $\chi_{\Delta}(\mathcal{G}_H^{\text{odd}})$ and the clustered chromatic number $\chi_{\star}(\mathcal{G}_H^{\text{odd}})$ equal $\overline{\operatorname{td}}(H)-1$, closing the exponential gap between the known lower bound $\overline{\operatorname{td}}(H)-1$ and the upper bound $3 \cdot 2^{\overline{\operatorname{td}}(H)}-4$ established in Theorem 1. No follow-up paper proving or disproving this conjecture was found in a wide web search through May 2026; the conjecture remains open.
search_queries: odd minor free graphs clustered chromatic number defective treedepth 2308.15721
odd H-minor-free clustered colouring defective chromatic number Hickingbotham Wood
site:arxiv.org "2308.15721" clustered chromatic odd minor treedepth conjecture 2025 2026
notes: No follow-up found after 5 web calls. The conjecture is recent (published October 2024) and posits that the exponential upper bound in Theorem 1 can be replaced by the linear lower bound td(H)-1. Semantic Scholar citation lookup for arXiv:2308.15721 returned no relevant citing papers addressing Conjecture 2. The related paper arXiv:2404.14940 (Brianski et al., defective/clustered colouring for graphs with given girth) does not appear to address the odd-minor setting of this conjecture.
